raw.reasoning_summary: Tag OpenSSL_1_0_2a is AFFECTED by CVE-2023-0464. The fix (commit 2017771e) is not an ancestor of this tag. The X509_POLICY_TREE_st struct at crypto/x509v3/pcy_int.h:115 lacks node_count and node_maximum fields. The level_add_node() function at crypto/x509v3/pcy_node.c:57 has no bounds check on node count, allowing unbounded policy tree growth via crafted certificate chains.

evidence_snippets.snippet: struct X509_POLICY_TREE_st { X509_POLICY_LEVEL *levels; int nlevel; STACK_OF(X509_POLICY_DATA) *extra_data; STACK_OF(X509_POLICY_NODE) *auth_policies; STACK_OF(X509_POLICY_NODE) *user_policies; unsigned int flags; };

X509_POLICY_NODE *level_add_node(X509_POLICY_LEVEL *level, const X509_POLICY_DATA *data, X509_POLICY_NODE *parent, X509_POLICY_TREE *tree) { X509_POLICY_NODE *node; node = OPENSSL_malloc(sizeof(X509_POLICY_NODE)); if (!node) return NULL; ... }
Fix 2017771e2d is NOT an ancestor of OpenSSL_1_0_0

raw.reasoning_summary: OpenSSL_1_0_0 predates fix 2017771e2d; X509_POLICY_TREE_st lacks node_count/node_maximum fields, level_add_node() performs unbounded malloc without any node-count check, and no OPENSSL_POLICY_TREE_NODES_MAX limit is defined. The vulnerability (CWE-295: improper resource-constrained certificate policy validation) is fully present.

evidence_snippets.snippet: No OPENSSL_POLICY_TREE_NODES_MAX macro, no tree->node_maximum initialization in tree_init()

matched_predicates: X509_POLICY_TREE_st lacks node_count/node_maximum fields: pcy_int.h:130-146 no size-limiting fields
level_add_node lacks node-count guard: pcy_node.c:130-166 no tree->node_count >= tree->node_maximum check
tree_init lacks OPENSSL_POLICY_TREE_NODES_MAX: pcy_tree.c no node_maximum initialization
raw.reasoning_summary: OpenSSL_1_0_2j is AFFECTED by CVE-2023-0464. The fix commit 2017771e2db3 adds node_count/node_maximum fields to X509_POLICY_TREE_st and a guard in level_add_node limiting tree growth to 1000 nodes (OPENSSL_POLICY_TREE_NODES_MAX). At tag OpenSSL_1_0_2j, the struct (crypto/x509v3/pcy_int.h:130-146) lacks these fields, level_add_node (crypto/x509v3/pcy_node.c:130-166) adds nodes with no bound check, and tree_init (crypto/x509v3/pcy_tree.c) sets no maximum. The fix commit is not an ancestor of the tag (verified via merge-base and tag --contains). Without these protections, an attacker can trigger unbounded policy tree growth via a crafted certificate chain, causing denial of service.

evidence_snippets.snippet: merge-base --is-ancestor returned NO: fix commit 2017771e2db3e2b96f89bbe8766c3209f6a99545 is NOT an ancestor of OpenSSL_1_0_1

matched_predicates: root_cause: X509_POLICY_TREE_st lacks node_count/node_maximum fields (pcy_int.h:struct X509_POLICY_TREE_st)
fix_not_applied: level_add_node has no node-maximum guard before allocating new nodes (pcy_node.c:112-123)
fix_not_applied: tree_init does not set node_maximum or define OPENSSL_POLICY_TREE_NODES_MAX (pcy_tree.c:tree_init)
fix_not_ancestor: fix commit 2017771e2db3e2b96f89bbe8766c3209f6a99545 is not an ancestor of OpenSSL_1_0_1
raw.reasoning_summary: OpenSSL_1_0_1 is AFFECTED by CVE-2023-0464. The X509_POLICY_TREE_st struct in crypto/x509v3/pcy_int.h completely lacks the node_count and node_maximum fields introduced by the fix. The level_add_node function in pcy_node.c has no node-count cap guard. The tree_init function in pcy_tree.c does not set any node maximum. Fix commit 2017771e2d is not an ancestor. All three fix-touched files exist at crypto/x509v3/ (pre-rename paths) with the vulnerable code present and the fix absent.
